262: // 4. PI-A2: DEGRADATION TYPES

269:   reason: 'low_quality' | 'insufficient_results' | 'both';

271:   resultCount: number;

276: interface DegradationEvent {

283: /**
284:  * Absolute quality floor for degradation checks.

287:  * high fixed threshold causes false degradations. Use a conservative floor and
288:  * pair it with a relative-gap check to avoid score-scale coupling.
289:  */

406:   try {
407:     if (shouldUseSqliteLexicalEngine(db)) {
408:       return ftsSearch(query, {
409:         limit,
410:         specFolder,
411:       }).map((result) => ({
412:         ...result,
413:         bm25_score: result.score,
414:         source: 'bm25',
415:         sources: Array.from(new Set([
416:           ...(
417:             Array.isArray((result as Record<string, unknown>).sources)
418:               ? ((result as Record<string, unknown>).sources as unknown[]).filter((source): source is string => typeof source === 'string')
419:               : []
420:           ),
421:           'fts',
422:           'bm25',
423:         ])),
424:       }));
425:     }
426: 
427:     const index = getIndex();
428:     const results = index.search(query, limit);
429: 
430:     // BM25 document IDs are stringified
431:     // Numeric memory IDs (e.g., "42"), not spec folder paths. The old filter compared
432:     // R.id against specFolder which never matched. Use DB lookup to resolve spec_folder.
433: 
434:     // Batch-resolve metadata for all result IDs (was N+1 individual queries)
435:     // SECURITY: Spec-folder scope MUST fail closed — any error in scope
436:     // resolution returns [] rather than leaking unscoped BM25 candidates.
437:     let memoryMetadataMap: Map<number, Bm25MemoryMetadata> | null = null;
438:     if (results.length > 0) {
439:       if (!db) {
440:         if (specFolder) {
441:           const error = new Error('Database unavailable for spec-folder scope lookup');
442:           console.warn('[BM25] Spec-folder scope lookup failed, returning empty scoped results:', error);
443:           return [];
444:         }
445:       } else {
446:         try {
447:           const ids = results.map((r: { id: string }) => Number(r.id));
448:           const placeholders = ids.map(() => '?').join(',');
449:           const rows = db.prepare(
450:             `SELECT id, spec_folder, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id IN (${placeholders})`
451:           ).all(...ids) as Array<{ id: number; spec_folder: string | null; importance_tier: string | null }>;
452:           memoryMetadataMap = new Map();
453:           for (const row of rows) {
454:             memoryMetadataMap.set(row.id, {
455:               specFolder: row.spec_folder,
456:               importanceTier: row.importance_tier,
457:             });
458:           }
459:         } catch (error: unknown) {
460:           console.warn('[BM25] Spec-folder scope lookup failed, returning empty scoped results:', error);
461:           return [];
462:         }
463:       }
464: 
465:       // DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH: If specFolder was requested but metadata resolution
466:       // is still null after the resolution block, something unexpected happened.
467:       // Fail closed rather than falling through to unscoped results.
468:       if (specFolder && !memoryMetadataMap) {
469:         const error = new Error('memoryMetadataMap unexpectedly null after scope resolution');
470:         console.warn('[BM25] Spec-folder scope lookup failed, returning empty scoped results:', error);
471:         return [];
472:       }
473:     }
474: 
475:     return results
476:       .filter((r: { id: string }) => {
477:         const metadata = memoryMetadataMap?.get(Number(r.id));
478:         if (metadata?.importanceTier === 'deprecated') return false;
479:         if (!specFolder) return true;
480:         if (!metadata) return false;
481:         const folder = metadata.specFolder;
482:         if (!folder) return false;
483:         return folder === specFolder || folder.startsWith(specFolder + '/');
484:       })
485:       .map((r: { id: string; score: number }) => ({
486:         ...r,
487:         source: 'bm25',
488:       }));
489:   } catch (error: unknown) {
490:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
491:     console.warn(`[hybrid-search] BM25 search failed: ${msg}`);
492:     return [];
493:   }
494: }
495: 
496: /**
497:  * Check whether the BM25 index is populated and available for search.
498:  * @returns True if the BM25 index exists and contains at least one document.
499:  */
500: function isBm25Available(): boolean {
501:   if (!isBm25Enabled()) {
502:     return false;
503:   }
504: 
505:   try {
506:     const index = getIndex();
507:     return index.getStats().documentCount > 0;
508:   } catch (_err: unknown) {
509:     // Swallow index-not-initialized errors; caller treats absence as unavailable
510:     return false;
511:   }
512: }

514: // 9. FTS SEARCH
515: 
516: /**
517:  * Check whether the FTS5 full-text search table exists in the database.
518:  * @returns True if the memory_fts table exists in the connected database.
519:  */
520: function isFtsAvailable(): boolean {
521:   if (!db) {
522:     console.warn('[hybrid-search] db not initialized — isFtsAvailable returning false');
523:     return false;
524:   }
525: 
526:   try {
527:     const result = (db.prepare(`
528:       SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
529:     `) as Database.Statement).get() as { name: string } | undefined;
530:     return !!result;
531:   } catch (_err: unknown) {
532:     // Swallow DB errors; caller treats absence as unavailable
533:     return false;
534:   }
535: }

537: /**
538:  * Run FTS5 full-text search with weighted BM25 scoring and optional spec folder filtering.
539:  * @param query - The search query string.
540:  * @param options - Optional limit, specFolder filter, and includeArchived flag.
541:  * @returns Array of FTS-scored results tagged with source 'fts'.
542:  */
543: function ftsSearch(
544:   query: string,
545:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string; includeArchived?: boolean } = {}
546: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
547:   if (!db || !isFtsAvailable()) {
548:     console.warn('[hybrid-search] db not initialized or FTS unavailable — returning empty ftsSearch results');
549:     return [];
550:   }
551: 
552:   const { limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT, specFolder, includeArchived = false } = options;
553: 
554:   try {
555:     // Delegate to weighted BM25 FTS5 search from sqlite-fts.ts
556:     // Uses bm25(memory_fts, 10.0, 5.0, 2.0, 1.0) for per-column weighting
557:     // (title 10x, trigger_phrases 5x, file_path 2x, content 1x)
558:     // Filters: deprecated-tier exclusion and spec-folder matching handled by fts5Bm25Search
559:     const bm25Results = fts5Bm25Search(db, query, { limit, specFolder, includeArchived });
560: 
561:     return bm25Results.map(row => ({
562:       ...row,
563:       id: row.id as number,
564:       score: row.fts_score || 0,
565:       source: 'fts',
566:     }));
567:   } catch (error: unknown) {
568:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
569:     console.warn(`[hybrid-search] FTS search failed: ${msg}`);
570:     return [];
571:   }
572: }
573: 
574: // 10. COMBINED LEXICAL SEARCH
575: 
576: /**
577:  * Merge FTS and BM25 search results, deduplicating by ID and preferring FTS scores.
578:  * @param query - The search query string.
579:  * @param options - Optional limit, specFolder filter, and includeArchived flag.
580:  * @returns Deduplicated array of merged results sorted by score descending.
581:  */
582: function combinedLexicalSearch(
583:   query: string,
584:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string; includeArchived?: boolean } = {}
585: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
586:   const ftsResults = ftsSearch(query, options);
587:   const bm25Results = bm25Search(query, options);
588: 
589:   // Merge by canonical ID, prefer FTS scores.
590:   // CanonicalResultId() prevents duplicate rows when one channel emits
591:   // Numeric IDs (42) and another emits string IDs ("42" or "mem:42").
592:   const merged = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
593: 
594:   for (const r of ftsResults) {
595:     merged.set(canonicalResultId(r.id), r);
596:   }
597: 
598:   for (const r of bm25Results) {
599:     const canonicalId = canonicalResultId(r.id);
600:     if (!merged.has(canonicalId)) {
601:       merged.set(canonicalId, r);
602:     }
603:   }
604: 
605:   return Array.from(merged.values())
606:     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
607:     .slice(0, options.limit || DEFAULT_LIMIT);
608: }
609: 
610: function normalizeTriggerText(value: string): string {
611:   return value
612:     .toLowerCase()
613:     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, ' ')
614:     .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
615:     .trim();
616: }
617: 
618: function triggerQueryTokens(query: string): string[] {
619:   return Array.from(new Set(
620:     normalizeTriggerText(query)
621:       .split(/\s+/)
622:       .filter((token) => token.length >= 2)
623:   ));
624: }
625: 
626: function computeTriggerMatchScore(query: string, phrase: string): number {
627:   const normalizedQuery = normalizeTriggerText(query);
628:   const normalizedPhrase = normalizeTriggerText(phrase);
629:   if (!normalizedQuery || !normalizedPhrase) return 0;
630:   if (normalizedPhrase === normalizedQuery) return 1.0;
631: 
632:   const queryTokens = triggerQueryTokens(normalizedQuery);
633:   const phraseTokens = new Set(triggerQueryTokens(normalizedPhrase));
634:   if (phraseTokens.size < 2) return 0;
635:   if (normalizedPhrase.includes(normalizedQuery)) return 0.94;
636:   if (normalizedQuery.includes(normalizedPhrase)) return 0.88;
637:   if (queryTokens.length === 0) return 0;
638:   const overlap = queryTokens.filter((token) => phraseTokens.has(token)).length;
639:   const coverage = overlap / queryTokens.length;
640:   return coverage >= 0.8 ? coverage * 0.75 : 0;
641: }
642: 
643: function timestampBoost(value: unknown): number {
644:   if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.length === 0) return 0;
645:   const parsed = Date.parse(value);
646:   if (!Number.isFinite(parsed)) return 0;
647:   const ageDays = Math.max(0, (Date.now() - parsed) / 86_400_000);
648:   return 1 / (1 + ageDays / 30);
649: }
650: 
651: function exactTriggerSearch(
652:   query: string,
653:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string; includeArchived?: boolean } = {}
654: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
655:   if (!db) return [];
656: 
657:   const tokens = triggerQueryTokens(query).filter((token) => token.length >= 3).slice(0, 8);
658:   if (tokens.length === 0) return [];
659: 
660:   const { limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT, specFolder } = options;
661:   const normalizedQuery = normalizeTriggerText(query);
662:   const exactPhraseLike = `%${normalizedQuery}%`;
663:   const candidateLimit = Math.max(200, Math.min(1000, limit * 100));
664:   const conditions = [
665:     `m.trigger_phrases IS NOT NULL`,
666:     `m.trigger_phrases != ''`,
667:     `m.trigger_phrases != '[]'`,
668:     `(m.importance_tier IS NULL OR m.importance_tier != 'deprecated')`,
669:     `(m.expires_at IS NULL OR m.expires_at > datetime('now'))`,
670:     `(${tokens.map(() => 'LOWER(m.trigger_phrases) LIKE ?').join(' OR ')})`,
671:   ];
672:   const params: unknown[] = tokens.map((token) => `%${token}%`);
673: 
674:   if (specFolder) {
675:     conditions.push(`(m.spec_folder = ? OR m.spec_folder LIKE ?)`);
676:     params.push(specFolder, `${specFolder}/%`);
677:   }
678: 
679:   try {
680:     const rows = db.prepare(`
681:       SELECT m.*
682:       FROM memory_index m
683:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
684:       WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}
685:       ORDER BY
686:         CASE WHEN LOWER(m.trigger_phrases) LIKE ? THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
687:         COALESCE(m.updated_at, m.created_at, '') DESC
688:       LIMIT ${candidateLimit}
689:     `).all(...params, exactPhraseLike) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
690: 
691:     return rows
692:       .map((row): HybridSearchResult | null => {
693:         const phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases as string | string[] | null | undefined);
694:         const matchScore = Math.max(0, ...phrases.map((phrase) => computeTriggerMatchScore(query, phrase)));
695:         if (matchScore <= 0) return null;
696: 
697:         const importance = typeof row.importance_weight === 'number' && Number.isFinite(row.importance_weight)
698:           ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, row.importance_weight))
699:           : 0.5;
700:         const recency = timestampBoost(row.updated_at ?? row.created_at);
701:         const score = Math.min(1, matchScore + importance * 0.03 + recency * 0.04);
702: 
703:         return {
704:           ...row,
705:           id: row.id as number,
706:           score,
707:           triggerScore: matchScore,
708:           source: 'trigger',
709:         };
710:       })
711:       .filter((row): row is HybridSearchResult => row !== null)
712:       .sort((a, b) => {
713:         const scoreDelta = b.score - a.score;
714:         if (scoreDelta !== 0) return scoreDelta;
715:         const bTime = Date.parse(String(b.updated_at ?? b.created_at ?? '')) || 0;
716:         const aTime = Date.parse(String(a.updated_at ?? a.created_at ?? '')) || 0;
717:         return bTime - aTime;
718:       })
719:       .slice(0, limit);
720:   } catch (error: unknown) {
721:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
722:     console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Trigger phrase search failed: ${msg}`);
723:     return [];
724:   }
725: }
726: 
727: type RawChannelList = {
728:   source: string;
729:   results: Array<{ id: number | string; [key: string]: unknown }>;
730: };
731: 
732: function resolveRawCandidateScore(result: { score?: unknown; similarity?: unknown }): number {
733:   if (typeof result.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.score)) {
734:     return result.score;
735:   }
736:   if (typeof result.similarity === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.similarity)) {
737:     return result.similarity / 100;
738:   }
739:   return 0;
740: }
741: 
742: function getCandidateSources(result: HybridSearchResult): string[] {
743:   const sourceList = (result as { sources?: unknown }).sources;
744:   if (Array.isArray(sourceList)) {
745:     return sourceList.filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string');
746:   }
747:   return typeof result.source === 'string' && result.source.length > 0
748:     ? [result.source]
749:     : [];
750: }
751: 
752: function getCandidateSourceScores(result: HybridSearchResult): Record<string, number> {
753:   const sourceScores = (result as { sourceScores?: unknown }).sourceScores;
754:   if (sourceScores && typeof sourceScores === 'object' && !Array.isArray(sourceScores)) {
755:     const normalizedScores: Record<string, number> = {};
756:     for (const [source, score] of Object.entries(sourceScores as Record<string, unknown>)) {
757:       if (typeof score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(score)) {
758:         normalizedScores[source] = score;
759:       }
760:     }
761:     return normalizedScores;
762:   }
763: 
764:   const fallbackScores: Record<string, number> = {};
765:   const resultScore = typeof result.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.score)
766:     ? result.score
767:     : 0;
768:   for (const source of getCandidateSources(result)) {
769:     fallbackScores[source] = resultScore;
770:   }
771:   return fallbackScores;
772: }
773: 
774: function mergeRawCandidate(
775:   existing: HybridSearchResult | undefined,
776:   incoming: HybridSearchResult
777: ): HybridSearchResult {
778:   if (!existing) {
779:     const sources = getCandidateSources(incoming);
780:     const channelCount = sources.length;
781:     return {
782:       ...incoming,
783:       sources,
784:       channelCount,
785:       sourceScores: getCandidateSourceScores(incoming),
786:       channelAttribution: sources,
787:     };
788:   }
789: 
790:   const existingScore = typeof existing.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(existing.score)
791:     ? existing.score
792:     : 0;
793:   const incomingScore = typeof incoming.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(incoming.score)
794:     ? incoming.score
795:     : 0;
796:   const primary = incomingScore > existingScore ? incoming : existing;
797:   const secondary = primary === incoming ? existing : incoming;
798:   const sources = Array.from(new Set([
799:     ...getCandidateSources(existing),
800:     ...getCandidateSources(incoming),
801:   ]));
802:   const channelCount = sources.length;
803:   const crossChannelBonus = Math.min(0.06, 0.02 * Math.max(0, channelCount - 1));
804:   const mergedScore = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, Math.max(existingScore, incomingScore) + crossChannelBonus));
805: 
806:   return {
807:     ...(secondary ?? {}),
808:     ...primary,
809:     score: mergedScore,
810:     source: typeof primary.source === 'string' ? primary.source : (sources[0] ?? 'hybrid'),
811:     sources,
812:     channelCount,
813:     sourceScores: {
814:       ...getCandidateSourceScores(existing),
815:       ...getCandidateSourceScores(incoming),
816:     },
817:     channelAttribution: sources,
818:   };
819: }
820: 
821: function collectCandidatesFromLists(
822:   lists: RawChannelList[],
823:   limit?: number
824: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
825:   const deduped = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
826: 
827:   for (const list of lists) {
828:     if (!Array.isArray(list.results) || list.results.length === 0) continue;
829: 
830:     const scored = list.results.map((result) => {
831:       const rawScore = resolveRawCandidateScore(result as { score?: unknown; similarity?: unknown });
832:       return { result, rawScore };
833:     });
834: 
835:     let min = Infinity;
836:     let max = -Infinity;
837:     for (const { rawScore } of scored) {
838:       if (rawScore < min) min = rawScore;
839:       if (rawScore > max) max = rawScore;
840:     }
841:     const range = max - min;
842: 
843:     for (const { result, rawScore } of scored) {
844:       const normalizedScore = range > 0 ? (rawScore - min) / range : (rawScore > 0 ? 1.0 : 0);
845:       const candidate: HybridSearchResult = {
846:         ...result,
847:         id: result.id,
848:         source: list.source,
849:         score: normalizedScore,
850:         sources: [list.source],
851:         sourceScores: { [list.source]: normalizedScore },
852:         channelAttribution: [list.source],
853:       };
854:       const key = canonicalResultId(candidate.id);
855:       deduped.set(key, mergeRawCandidate(deduped.get(key), candidate));
856:     }
857:   }
858: 
859:   return applyResultLimit(
860:     Array.from(deduped.values()).sort((a, b) => {
861:       const aScore = typeof a.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(a.score) ? a.score : 0;
862:       const bScore = typeof b.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(b.score) ? b.score : 0;
863:       return bScore - aScore;
864:     }),
865:     limit
866:   );
867: }
868: 
869: function mergeRawCandidateSets(
870:   existing: HybridSearchResult[],
871:   incoming: HybridSearchResult[],
872:   limit?: number
873: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
874:   const merged = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
875: 
876:   for (const result of [...existing, ...incoming]) {
877:     const key = canonicalResultId(result.id);
878:     merged.set(key, mergeRawCandidate(merged.get(key), result));
879:   }
880: 
881:   return applyResultLimit(
882:     Array.from(merged.values()).sort((a, b) => {
883:       const aScore = typeof a.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(a.score) ? a.score : 0;
884:       const bScore = typeof b.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(b.score) ? b.score : 0;
885:       return bScore - aScore;
886:     }),
887:     limit
888:   );
889: }

891: function getAllowedChannels(options: HybridSearchOptions): Set<ChannelName> {
892:   const allowed = new Set<ChannelName>(['vector', 'fts', 'graph', 'degree']);
893: 
894:   if (isBm25Enabled()) {
895:     allowed.add('bm25');
896:   }
897: 
898:   if (options.useVector === false) allowed.delete('vector');
899:   if (options.useBm25 === false) allowed.delete('bm25');
900:   if (options.useFts === false) allowed.delete('fts');
901:   if (options.useGraph === false) {
902:     allowed.delete('graph');
903:     allowed.delete('degree');
904:   }
905: 
906:   return allowed;
907: }

909: function applyAllowedChannelOverrides(
910:   options: HybridSearchOptions,
911:   allowedChannels: Set<ChannelName>,
912:   overrides: Partial<HybridSearchOptions> = {}
913: ): HybridSearchOptions {
914:   return {
915:     ...options,
916:     ...overrides,
917:     useVector: allowedChannels.has('vector'),
918:     useBm25: allowedChannels.has('bm25'),
919:     useFts: allowedChannels.has('fts'),
920:     useGraph: allowedChannels.has('graph'),
921:   };
922: }

932:   trigger?: 'empty' | DegradationTrigger;
933: }

941:   evaluationMode: boolean;
942:   intent: string;
943:   lists: Array<{
944:     source: string;
945:     results: Array<{ id: number | string; [key: string]: unknown }>;
946:     weight?: number;
947:   }>;
948:   routeResult: ReturnType<typeof routeQuery>;
949:   budgetResult: ReturnType<typeof getDynamicTokenBudget>;
950:   s3meta: Sprint3PipelineMeta;

955: function resolveAdaptiveDocumentType(
956:   lists: Array<{ results: Array<Record<string, unknown>> }>,
957: ): string | undefined {
958:   const counts = new Map<string, number>();
959:   for (const list of lists) {
960:     for (const result of list.results.slice(0, 3)) {
961:       const candidate = result.documentType ?? result.document_type;
962:       if (typeof candidate !== 'string' || candidate.length === 0) {
963:         continue;
964:       }
965:       counts.set(candidate, (counts.get(candidate) ?? 0) + 1);
966:     }
967:   }
968: 
969:   let topType: string | undefined;
970:   let topCount = 0;
971:   for (const [documentType, count] of counts) {
972:     if (count > topCount) {
973:       topType = documentType;
974:       topCount = count;
975:     }
976:   }
977: 
978:   return topType;
979: }

981: function markFallbackRetry(results: HybridSearchResult[]): HybridSearchResult[] {
982:   for (const result of results) {
983:     (result as Record<string, unknown>).fallbackRetry = true;
984:   }
985: 
986:   return results;
987: }

989: function toEmbeddingBufferView(value: unknown): Float32Array | null {
990:   if (value instanceof Float32Array) {
991:     return value;
992:   }
993: 
994:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
995:     const asNumbers = value.every((entry) => typeof entry === 'number' && Number.isFinite(entry));
996:     return asNumbers ? new Float32Array(value) : null;
997:   }
998: 
999:   if (Buffer.isBuffer(value)) {
1000:     return new Float32Array(value.buffer, value.byteOffset, value.byteLength / 4);
1001:   }
1002: 
1003:   return null;
1004: }

1006: async function executeFallbackPlan(
1007:   query: string,
1008:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1009:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {},
1010:   planKind: FallbackPlanKind,
1011:   overrides: Partial<HybridSearchOptions> = {}
1012: ): Promise<FallbackPlanExecution> {
1013:   const allowedChannels = getAllowedChannels(options);
1014:   const stages: FallbackPlanStage[] = [];
1015: 
1016:   const primaryOptions = applyAllowedChannelOverrides(options, allowedChannels, {
1017:     minSimilarity: options.minSimilarity ?? PRIMARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY,
1018:     ...overrides,
1019:   });
1020:   const primaryExecution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, primaryOptions);
1021:   const primaryResults = primaryExecution
1022:     ? applyResultLimit(primaryExecution.fusedResults, primaryOptions.limit)
1023:     : await hybridSearch(query, embedding, primaryOptions);
1024:   stages.push({
1025:     stage: 'primary',
1026:     options: primaryOptions,
1027:     results: primaryResults,
1028:     execution: primaryExecution,
1029:   });
1030: 
1031:   if (planKind === 'tiered') {
1032:     const trigger = checkDegradation(primaryResults);
1033:     if (!trigger) {
1034:       return { allowedChannels, stages };
1035:     }
1036: 
1037:     const retryOptions = applyAllowedChannelOverrides(options, allowedChannels, {
1038:       ...overrides,
1039:       minSimilarity: TIERED_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY,
1040:       forceAllChannels: true,
1041:     });
1042:     const retryExecution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1043:     const retryResults = retryExecution
1044:       ? applyResultLimit(retryExecution.fusedResults, retryOptions.limit)
1045:       : await hybridSearch(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1046:     stages.push({
1047:       stage: 'retry',
1048:       options: retryOptions,
1049:       results: retryResults,
1050:       execution: retryExecution,
1051:       trigger,
1052:     });
1053:     return { allowedChannels, stages };
1054:   }
1055: 
1056:   const primaryMinSimilarity = primaryOptions.minSimilarity ?? PRIMARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY;
1057:   if (primaryResults.length === 0 && primaryMinSimilarity >= SECONDARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY) {
1058:     const retryOptions = applyAllowedChannelOverrides(options, allowedChannels, {
1059:       ...overrides,
1060:       minSimilarity: SECONDARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY,
1061:     });

1063:     const retryResultsBase = retryExecution
1064:       ? applyResultLimit(retryExecution.fusedResults, retryOptions.limit)
1065:       : await hybridSearch(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1066:     const retryResults = retryResultsBase.length > 0
1067:       ? markFallbackRetry(retryResultsBase)
1068:       : retryResultsBase;

1070:       stage: 'retry',
1071:       options: retryOptions,

1081: // 11. HYBRID SEARCH

1083: /**
1084:  * Run multi-channel hybrid search combining vector, FTS, BM25, and graph results with per-source normalization.
1085:  * Prefer hybridSearchEnhanced() or searchWithFallback() instead. This function uses naive per-source
1086:  * min-max normalization which produces different orderings than the RRF pipeline in hybridSearchEnhanced().
1087:  * Retained as internal fallback only.
1088:  */
1089: async function hybridSearch(
1090:   query: string,
1091:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1092:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
1093: ): Promise<HybridSearchResult[]> {
1094:   const {
1095:     limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT,
1096:     specFolder,
1097:     minSimilarity = 0,
1098:     useBm25 = isBm25Enabled(),
1099:     useFts = true,
1100:     useVector = true,
1101:     useGraph = true,
1102:     includeArchived = false,
1103:   } = options;
1104: 
1105:   const results: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1106: 
1107:   // Vector search
1108:   if (useVector && embedding && vectorSearchFn) {
1109:     try {
1110:       const vectorResults = vectorSearchFn(embedding, {
1111:         limit,
1112:         specFolder,
1113:         minSimilarity,
1114:         includeConstitutional: false, // Handler manages constitutional separately
1115:         includeArchived,
1116:       });
1117:       for (const r of vectorResults) {
1118:         results.push({
1119:           ...r,
1120:           id: r.id as number,
1121:           score: (r.similarity as number) || 0,
1122:           source: 'vector',
1123:         });
1124:       }
1125:     } catch (error: unknown) {
1126:       const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1127:       console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Vector search failed: ${msg}`);
1128:     }
1129:   }
1130: 
1131:   // FTS search
1132:   if (useFts) {
1133:     const ftsResults = ftsSearch(query, { limit, specFolder, includeArchived });
1134:     results.push(...ftsResults);
1135:   }
1136: 
1137:   // BM25 search
1138:   if (useBm25) {
1139:     const bm25Results = bm25Search(query, { limit, specFolder });
1140:     results.push(...bm25Results);
1141:   }
1142: 
1143:   // Graph search
1144:   if (useGraph && graphSearchFn) {
1145:     try {
1146:       const graphResults = graphSearchFn(query, { limit, specFolder });
1147:       for (const r of graphResults) {
1148:         results.push({
1149:           ...r,
1150:           id: r.id as number | string,
1151:           score: (r.score as number) || 0,
1152:           source: 'graph',
1153:         });
1154:       }
1155:     } catch (error: unknown) {
1156:       const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1157:       console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Graph search failed: ${msg}`);
1158:     }
1159:   }
1160: 
1161:   // Normalize scores per source before merging so one method's raw scale
1162:   // Does not dominate others during final ranking.
1163:   const bySource = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult[]>();
1164:   for (const r of results) {
1165:     const src = r.source || 'unknown';
1166:     if (!bySource.has(src)) bySource.set(src, []);
1167:     bySource.get(src)!.push(r); // non-null safe: has() guard above guarantees entry exists
1168:   }
1169: 
1170:   const normalized: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1171:   for (const [, group] of bySource) {
1172:     if (group.length === 0) continue;
1173:     const scores = group.map(r => r.score);
1174:     let min = Infinity;
1175:     let max = -Infinity;
1176:     for (const s of scores) {
1177:       if (s < min) min = s;
1178:       if (s > max) max = s;
1179:     }
1180:     const range = max - min;
1181:     for (const r of group) {
1182:       normalized.push({
1183:         ...r,
1184:         score: range > 0 ? (r.score - min) / range : (r.score > 0 ? 1.0 : 0),
1185:       });
1186:     }
1187:   }
1188: 
1189:   // Deduplicate by ID (keep highest normalized score)
1190:   // LIMITATION: When a result appears in multiple sources (e.g., vector + fts),
1191:   // Only the highest-scoring entry's `source` is preserved. Multi-source provenance
1192:   // Is lost here. To fix properly, HybridSearchResult would need a `sources: string[]`
1193:   // Field and downstream consumers would need to be updated accordingly.
1194:   const deduped = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
1195:   for (const r of normalized) {
1196:     const canonicalId = canonicalResultId(r.id);
1197:     const existing = deduped.get(canonicalId);
1198:     if (!existing || r.score > existing.score) {
1199:       deduped.set(canonicalId, r);
1200:     }
1201:   }
1202: 
1203:   return Array.from(deduped.values())
1204:     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
1205:     .slice(0, limit);
1206: }

1209:  * Enhanced hybrid search with RRF fusion.
1210:  * All search channels use synchronous better-sqlite3; sequential execution
1211:  * is correct — Promise.all would add overhead without achieving parallelism.
1212:  */
1213: async function hybridSearchEnhanced(
1214:   query: string,
1215:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1216:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
1217: ): Promise<HybridSearchResult[]> {
1218:   const execution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, options);
1219:   if (execution) {
1220:     if (options.stopAfterFusion) {
1221:       return applyResultLimit(execution.fusedResults, options.limit);
1222:     }
1223: 
1224:     return enrichFusedResults(query, execution, options);
1225:   }
1226: 
1227:   return hybridSearch(query, embedding, options);
1228: }

2: // MODULE: Confidence Scoring
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Per-result calibrated confidence scoring
5: //
6: // PURPOSE: Combine margin, multi-channel agreement,
7: // and anchor density into a single calibrated confidence score per
8: // result. V1 uses heuristic scoring only — no LLM calls in the hot path.
9: //
10: // FEATURE FLAG: SPECKIT_RESULT_CONFIDENCE_V1 (default ON, graduated)
11: //
12: // OUTPUT SHAPE (per result):
13: // {
14: //   "confidence": {
15: //     "label": "high" | "medium" | "low",
16: //     "value": 0.78,
17: //     "drivers": ["large_margin", "multi_channel_agreement"]
18: //   },
19: //   "requestQuality": {
20: //     "label": "good" | "weak" | "gap"
21: //   }
22: // }
23: //
24: // IMPORTANT: This module only models ranking confidence for retrieval ordering.
25: // It is not freshness authority and it is not a substitute for StructuralTrust.
26: import { resolveEffectiveScore, type PipelineRow } from './pipeline/types.js';
27: 
28: declare const rankingConfidenceBrand: unique symbol;
29: 
30: // -- Constants --
31: 
32: const HIGH_THRESHOLD = 0.7;
33: const LOW_THRESHOLD = 0.4;
34: 
35: // Weights for each active confidence factor. The former reranker weight (0.20)
36: // was removed with the LLM reranker; its term was already inert (always 0), so
37: // rawValue stays capped at 0.80 exactly as before — behavior-neutral. The 0.20
38: // is intentionally NOT redistributed (that would change confidence scores).
39: const WEIGHT_MARGIN = 0.35;
40: const WEIGHT_CHANNEL_AGREEMENT = 0.30;
41: const WEIGHT_ANCHOR_DENSITY = 0.15;
42: 
43: // Margin thresholds (gap between top score and next score, 0–1 scale)
44: const LARGE_MARGIN_THRESHOLD = 0.15;
45: const SMALL_MARGIN_THRESHOLD = 0.05;
46: 
47: // Channel count thresholds
48: const STRONG_CHANNEL_AGREEMENT_MIN = 2;
49: 
50: // -- Types --
51: 
52: /** Confidence label for a single result. */
53: export type ConfidenceLabel = 'high' | 'medium' | 'low';
54: 
55: /**
56:  * Branded ranking score for retrieval ordering only.
57:  * Consumers must not reuse this as parser provenance, evidence status,
58:  * freshness authority, or any other StructuralTrust axis.
59:  */
60: export type RankingConfidenceValue = number & {
61:   readonly [rankingConfidenceBrand]: 'RankingConfidenceValue';

65: export type RequestQualityLabel = 'good' | 'weak' | 'gap';
66: 
67: /** Which factors drove the confidence score upward. */
68: export type ConfidenceDriver =
69:   | 'large_margin'
70:   | 'multi_channel_agreement'
71:   | 'anchor_density';
72: 
73: /** Per-result confidence payload. */
74: export interface RankingConfidenceContract {
75:   label: ConfidenceLabel;
76:   value: RankingConfidenceValue;
77:   drivers: ConfidenceDriver[];
78:   structuralTrust?: never;

127: 
128: /**
129:  * Count the number of unique channels that retrieved this result.
130:  * Inspects `sources`, `source`, and traceMetadata.attribution.
131:  */
132: function countChannels(result: ScoredResult): number {
133:   const channels = new Set<string>();
134: 
135:   if (Array.isArray(result.sources)) {
136:     for (const s of result.sources) {
137:       if (typeof s === 'string' && s.trim().length > 0) channels.add(s.trim());
138:     }
139:   }
140: 
141:   if (typeof result.source === 'string' && result.source.trim().length > 0) {
142:     channels.add(result.source.trim());
143:   }
144: 
145:   // Check traceMetadata.attribution for richer channel data
146:   const numericId = typeof result.id === 'number' ? result.id : null;
147:   const attribution = result.traceMetadata?.attribution;
148:   if (attribution && typeof attribution === 'object' && numericId !== null) {
149:     for (const [channel, memoryIds] of Object.entries(attribution as Record<string, unknown>)) {
150:       if (!Array.isArray(memoryIds)) continue;
151:       const matched = memoryIds.some((mid) => {
152:         const n = typeof mid === 'number' ? mid : typeof mid === 'string' ? Number(mid) : NaN;
153:         return Number.isFinite(n) && n === numericId;
154:       });
155:       if (matched && typeof channel === 'string' && channel.trim().length > 0) {
156:         channels.add(channel.trim());
157:       }
158:     }
159:   }
160: 
161:   return channels.size;
162: }
163: 
164: /**
165:  * Count anchors present in this result's anchorMetadata array.
166:  * A result with multiple named anchors is considered "dense" and
167:  * more likely to be a high-quality, well-structured memory.
168:  */
169: function countAnchors(result: ScoredResult): number {
170:   if (!Array.isArray(result.anchorMetadata)) return 0;
171:   return result.anchorMetadata.length;
172: }
173: 
174: /**
175:  * Compute the score margin between result[i] and result[i+1].
176:  * Tail results have no successor, so they receive no synthetic margin boost.
177:  */
178: function computeMargin(score: number, nextScore: number | null): number {
179:   if (nextScore === null) return 0;
180:   const gap = score - nextScore;
181:   return Math.max(0, gap);
182: }
183: 
184: /**
185:  * Map raw confidence value to a coarse label.
186:  */
187: function toConfidenceLabel(value: number): ConfidenceLabel {
188:   if (value >= HIGH_THRESHOLD) return 'high';
189:   if (value >= LOW_THRESHOLD) return 'medium';
190:   return 'low';
191: }
192: 
193: export function asRankingConfidenceValue(value: number): RankingConfidenceValue {
194:   const clamped = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, value));
195:   return (Math.round(clamped * 1000) / 1000) as RankingConfidenceValue;
196: }
197: 
198: // -- Public API --
199: 
200: /**
201:  * Compute per-result confidence for a ranked list of results.
202:  *
203:  * Each result receives a confidence object derived from:
204:  *   - Score margin to the next result (35% weight)
205:  *   - Number of channels that contributed this result (30%)
206:  *   - Anchor density in anchorMetadata (15%)
207:  *
208:  * @param results - Ranked results (highest score first). Ordering is assumed.
209:  * @returns Array of ResultConfidence objects, parallel to `results`.
210:  */
211: export function computeResultConfidence(results: ScoredResult[]): ResultConfidence[] {
212:   if (!Array.isArray(results) || results.length === 0) return [];
213: 
214:   const scores = results.map(resolveScore);
215: 
216:   return results.map((result, i): ResultConfidence => {
217:     const score = scores[i] ?? 0;
218:     const nextScore = i + 1 < scores.length ? (scores[i + 1] ?? null) : null;
219: 
220:     const margin = computeMargin(score, nextScore);
221:     const channelCount = countChannels(result);
222:     const anchorCount = countAnchors(result);
223: 
224:     // Factor scores (each 0–1)
225:     const marginFactor = margin >= LARGE_MARGIN_THRESHOLD
226:       ? 1.0
227:       : margin >= SMALL_MARGIN_THRESHOLD
228:         ? margin / LARGE_MARGIN_THRESHOLD
229:         : 0;
230: 
231:     const channelFactor = channelCount >= STRONG_CHANNEL_AGREEMENT_MIN
232:       ? 1.0
233:       : channelCount === 1
234:         ? 0.5
235:         : 0;
236: 
237:     // Anchor density: 1 anchor → 0.3, 2 → 0.6, 3+ → 1.0
238:     const anchorFactor = Math.min(1, anchorCount * 0.33);
239: 
240:     const rawValue =
241:       WEIGHT_MARGIN * marginFactor +
242:       WEIGHT_CHANNEL_AGREEMENT * channelFactor +
243:       WEIGHT_ANCHOR_DENSITY * anchorFactor;
244: 
245:     // Base score is a strong prior: if the score itself is very high, confidence
246:     // should reflect that even when heuristic signals are weak.
247:     const scorePrior = score * 0.4;
248:     const heuristicValue = rawValue * 0.6;
249:     const value = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, heuristicValue + scorePrior));
250: 
251:     const drivers: ConfidenceDriver[] = [];
252:     if (margin >= LARGE_MARGIN_THRESHOLD) drivers.push('large_margin');
253:     if (channelCount >= STRONG_CHANNEL_AGREEMENT_MIN) drivers.push('multi_channel_agreement');
254:     if (anchorCount >= 2) drivers.push('anchor_density');
255: 
256:       return {
257:         confidence: {
258:           label: toConfidenceLabel(value),
259:           value: asRankingConfidenceValue(value),
260:           drivers,
261:         },
262:       };
263:   });
264: }
265: 
266: /**
267:  * Compute request-level quality assessment based on the overall result set.
268:  *
269:  * - "good":  most results have high/medium confidence and a healthy top score
270:  * - "weak":  results exist but signals are mixed or low
271:  * - "gap":   no results, or all results have low confidence
272:  *
273:  * @param results   - The scored results for the query.
274:  * @param confidences - Parallel confidence array from `computeResultConfidence`.
275:  */
276: export function assessRequestQuality(
277:   results: ScoredResult[],
278:   confidences: ResultConfidence[],
279: ): RequestQualityAssessment {
280:   if (!Array.isArray(results) || results.length === 0) {
281:     return { requestQuality: { label: 'gap' } };
282:   }
283: 
284:   const highOrMediumCount = confidences.filter(
285:     (c) => c.confidence.label === 'high' || c.confidence.label === 'medium',
286:   ).length;
287: 
288:   const topScore = resolveScore(results[0]);
289: 
290:   const qualityRatio = highOrMediumCount / results.length;
291: 
292:   if (topScore >= HIGH_THRESHOLD && qualityRatio >= 0.6) {
293:     return { requestQuality: { label: 'good' } };
294:   }
295:   if (results.length > 0 && (topScore >= LOW_THRESHOLD || qualityRatio >= 0.3)) {
296:     return { requestQuality: { label: 'weak' } };
297:   }
298:   return { requestQuality: { label: 'gap' } };
299: }
300: 
301: /**
302:  * Check whether the per-result confidence feature flag is enabled.
303:  * Default: ON (graduated). Set SPECKIT_RESULT_CONFIDENCE_V1=false to disable.
304:  */
305: export { isResultConfidenceEnabled } from './search-flags.js';

(End of file - total 305 lines)
</content>

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Content Normalizer
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Content-aware memory filename generation
5: // Smarter memory content generation
6: //
7: // Purpose: Normalize raw markdown content before it is passed to
8: // Embedding generation or BM25 indexing.  Raw markdown contains
9: // Structural noise (YAML frontmatter, HTML comment anchors, pipe
10: // Table syntax, fence markers, checkbox notation) that degrades
11: // The quality of semantic embeddings and keyword retrieval.
12: //
13: // Integration points (do NOT modify those files here — reference only):
14: //   - memory-parser.ts  ~line 159  : `content` is assigned from readFileWithEncoding()
15: // → wrap with normalizeContentForEmbedding() before passing to generateDocumentEmbedding()
16: //   - memory-save.ts    ~line 1093 : before generateDocumentEmbedding(parsed.content)
17: // → normalizeContentForEmbedding(parsed.content)
18: //   - bm25-index.ts     ~line 245  : where `content_text` is used for token building
19: // → normalizeContentForBM25(content_text)
20: //
21: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22: // 1. PRIMITIVE STRIP / NORMALIZE HELPERS
23: 
24: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
25: /**
26:  * Strip YAML frontmatter block from the top of a markdown file.
27:  *
28:  * Frontmatter is already parsed separately by memory-parser.ts into
29:  * structured fields (title, trigger_phrases, context_type, etc.), so
30:  * including it verbatim in the embedding text just adds repetitive
31:  * key-value noise.
32:  *
33:  * Matches the canonical `---\n…\n---` form that starts at the very
34:  * first character of the document.
35:  */
36: export function stripYamlFrontmatter(content: string): string {
37:   // Must start at position 0.  The closing `---` must be on its own line.
38:   // Without the `m` flag, `^` anchors to position 0 only (document start),
39:   // Preventing accidental matches on mid-document `---` (e.g. HR rules).
40:   return content.replace(/^---[\s\S]*?\n---\s*\n?/, '');
41: }
42: 
43: /**
44:  * Strip ANCHOR comment markers while preserving the text inside them.
45:  *
46:  * Examples removed:
47:  *   <!-- ANCHOR: state -->
48:  *   <!-- /ANCHOR: state -->
49:  *   <!-- ANCHOR:next-steps -->
50:  */
51: export function stripAnchors(content: string): string {
52:   // Opening and closing anchor tags — keep nothing (structural only)
53:   return content.replace(/<!--\s*\/?ANCHOR:[^>]*-->/gi, '');
54: }
55: 
56: /**
57:  * Strip all HTML comments.
58:  *
59:  * This catches structural markers not covered by stripAnchors(), e.g.:
60:  * <!-- gate: open -->
61:  *   <!-- TODO: remove -->
62:  *   <!-- prettier-ignore -->
63:  */
64: export function stripHtmlComments(content: string): string {
65:   // Non-greedy match to avoid swallowing multiple comments in one pass
66:   return content.replace(/<!--[\s\S]*?-->/g, '');
67: }
68: 
69: /**
70:  * Strip code-fence markers while retaining the code body.
71:  *
72:  * Before:
73:  *   ```typescript
74:  *   const x = 1;
75:  *   ```
76:  *
77:  * After:
78:  *   const x = 1;
79:  *
80:  * The language identifier on the opening fence is discarded because
81:  * tokens like "typescript" or "json" carry no semantic signal about
82:  * the stored knowledge.
83:  */
84: export function stripCodeFences(content: string): string {
85:   // Match ``` optionally followed by a language identifier, then any content,
86:   // Then a closing ```.  The 's' flag makes '.' match newlines.
87:   return content.replace(/^```[^\n]*\n([\s\S]*?)^```\s*$/gm, '$1');
88: }
89: 
90: /**
91:  * Convert markdown pipe tables to plain prose.
92:  *
93:  * Separator rows (e.g. `| --- | --- |`) are dropped entirely.
94:  * Data rows have their cell content extracted and joined with spaces.
95:  *
96:  * Before:
97:  *   | Tool | Purpose | Status |
98:  *   | ---- | ------- | ------ |
99:  *   | Grep | search  | active |
100:  *
101:  * After:
102:  *   Tool Purpose Status
103:  *   Grep search active
104:  */
105: export function normalizeMarkdownTables(content: string): string {
106:   const lines = content.split('\n');
107:   const result: string[] = [];
108: 
109:   for (const line of lines) {
110:     const trimmed = line.trim();
111: 
112:     // Separator row — pure dashes, colons, pipes, spaces
113:     if (/^\|[\s|:-]+\|$/.test(trimmed)) {
114:       continue;
115:     }
116: 
117:     // Data / header row starting and ending with pipe
118:     if (trimmed.startsWith('|') && trimmed.endsWith('|')) {
119:       const cells = trimmed
120:         .slice(1, -1)               // remove leading and trailing pipe
121:         .split('|')
122:         .map(cell => cell.trim())
123:         .filter(cell => cell.length > 0 && !/^[-:\s]+$/.test(cell)); // skip pure-separator cells
124: 
125:       if (cells.length > 0) {
126:         result.push(cells.join(' '));
127:       }
128:       continue;
129:     }
130: 
131:     result.push(line);
132:   }
133: 
134:   return result.join('\n');
135: }
136: 
137: /**
138:  * Normalize markdown list notation to clean prose tokens.
139:  *
140:  * Handles:
141:  * GitHub-style task checkboxes: `- [ ] [P] Implement…` → `[ ] [P] Implement…`
142:  *   - Checked checkboxes:            `- [x] Done item`             →  `[x] Done item`
143:  *   - Plain list bullets:            `- item` / `* item`           →  `item`
144:  *   - Ordered list numbers:          `1. item`                      →  `item`
145:  *
146:  * Leading whitespace (indentation for nested lists) is preserved so
147:  * that hierarchical structure is still represented via spacing.
148:  */
149: export function normalizeMarkdownLists(content: string): string {
150:   return content
151:     // Preserve checklist state markers while dropping the bullet itself.
152:     .replace(/^(\s*)[-*]\s+(\[[ xX]\])\s*/gm, '$1$2 ')
153:     // Plain unordered list bullets
154:     .replace(/^(\s*)[-*]\s+/gm, '$1')
155:     // Ordered list numbers
156:     .replace(/^(\s*)\d+\.\s+/gm, '$1');
157: }
158: 
159: /**
160:  * Convert markdown headings to plain section labels without hash marks.
161:  *
162:  * Before:  `## 3. SCOPE`
163:  * After:   `SCOPE`
164:  *
165:  * The numeric prefix (e.g. "3.") is also stripped because section
166:  * numbering carries no semantic meaning for retrieval.
167:  */
168: export function normalizeHeadings(content: string): string {
169:   return content.replace(
170:     /^#{1,6}\s+(?:\d+\.\s+)?(.+)$/gm,
171:     (_match, heading: string) => heading.trim()
172:   );
173: }
174: 
175: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
176: // 2. WHITESPACE CLEANUP
177: 
178: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
179: /**
180:  * Collapse runs of blank lines to a single blank line, and trim
181:  * leading / trailing whitespace from the whole document.
182:  *
183:  * This is applied as the final step in all public normalization
184:  * functions to ensure a clean, compact result.
185:  */
186: function collapseWhitespace(content: string): string {
187:   return content
188:     .replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n')   // 3+ consecutive newlines → 2
189:     .trim();
190: }
191: 
192: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
193: // 3. PUBLIC COMPOSITE FUNCTIONS
194: 
195: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
196: /**
197:  * Normalize markdown content for use in embedding generation.
198:  *
199:  * Applies the full normalization pipeline in order:
200:  *   1. Strip YAML frontmatter      — already parsed into structured fields
201:  *   2. Strip anchor markers        — structural noise
202:  *   3. Strip remaining HTML comments — structural noise
203:  *   4. Strip code-fence markers    — keep code body, drop ``` + lang id
204:  *   5. Normalize pipe tables       — extract cell text
205:  *   6. Normalize list notation     — drop bullet / checkbox syntax
206:  *   7. Normalize headings          — drop `##` hash marks
207:  *   8. Collapse excess whitespace  — final cleanup
208:  *
209:  * The result is a clean prose representation suitable for semantic
210:  * embedding models (current local defaults and compatible providers).
211:  *
212:  * Integration point:
213:  *   memory-parser.ts  ~line 159 after `readFileWithEncoding()`,
214:  *   memory-save.ts    ~line 1093 before `generateDocumentEmbedding()`.
215:  */
216: export function normalizeContentForEmbedding(content: string): string {
217:   if (!content || typeof content !== 'string') {
218:     return '';
219:   }
220: 
221:   let normalized = content;
222:   normalized = stripYamlFrontmatter(normalized);
223:   normalized = stripAnchors(normalized);
224:   normalized = stripHtmlComments(normalized);
225:   normalized = stripCodeFences(normalized);
226:   normalized = normalizeMarkdownTables(normalized);
227:   normalized = normalizeMarkdownLists(normalized);
228:   normalized = normalizeHeadings(normalized);
229:   normalized = collapseWhitespace(normalized);
230: 
231:   return normalized;
232: }
233: 
234: /**
235:  * Normalize markdown content for use in BM25 keyword indexing.
236:  *
237:  * BM25 benefits from the same structural noise removal as the
238:  * embedding pipeline.  Code fence markers (``` + lang id) are stripped
239:  * but the code body is preserved so that exact identifier tokens
240:  * (function names, class names, CLI flags) remain searchable.
241:  *
242:  * Pipeline:
243:  *   1. Strip YAML frontmatter
244:  *   2. Strip anchor markers
245:  *   3. Strip remaining HTML comments
246:  *   4. Strip code-fence markers only (keep body)
247:  *   5. Normalize pipe tables
248:  *   6. Normalize list notation
249:  *   7. Normalize headings
250:  *   8. Collapse excess whitespace
251:  *
252:  * Integration point:
253:  *   bm25-index.ts ~line 245 where `content_text` is used for token building.
254:  */
255: export function normalizeContentForBM25(content: string): string {
256:   // The BM25 pipeline currently delegates to the same normalization
257:   // Steps as the embedding pipeline (frontmatter, anchors, HTML
258:   // Comments, code fences, tables, lists, headings, whitespace).
259:   // A separate entry point exists so BM25-specific adjustments
260:   // (e.g. preserving backtick inline code, adding stemming hints)
261:   // Can diverge in the future without modifying the embedding path.
262:   return normalizeContentForEmbedding(content);
263: }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Anchor Chunker
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Anchor-aware chunk thinning
5: // Splits large memory files into chunks using ANCHOR tags as
6: // Natural boundaries. Falls back to structure-aware markdown
7: // Splitting when anchors are absent.
8: import { chunkMarkdown } from '@spec-kit/shared/lib/structure-aware-chunker';
9: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10:    1. TYPES
11: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
12: 
13: /**
14:  * Describes the AnchorChunk shape.
15:  */
16: export interface AnchorChunk {
17:   /** The text content of this chunk */
18:   content: string;
19:   /** Anchor IDs covered by this chunk (empty for fallback chunks) */
20:   anchorIds: string[];
21:   /** Human-readable label for this chunk */
22:   label: string;
23:   /** Approximate character count */
24:   charCount: number;
25: }
26: 
27: /**
28:  * Describes the ChunkingResult shape.
29:  */
30: export interface ChunkingResult {
31:   /** Strategy used: 'anchor' or 'structure' */
32:   strategy: 'anchor' | 'structure';
33:   /** The chunks produced */
34:   chunks: AnchorChunk[];
35:   /** Summary text for the parent record (first ~500 chars) */
36:   parentSummary: string;
37: }
38: 
39: interface AnchorSection {
40:   id: string | null;
41:   content: string;
42:   charCount: number;
43: }
44: 
45: interface ExtractedAnchorSections {
46:   sections: AnchorSection[];
47:   anchorCount: number;
48: }
49: 
50: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
51:    2. CONSTANTS
52: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
53: 
54: /** Target chunk size in characters (~1000 tokens at 4 chars/token) */
55: const TARGET_CHUNK_CHARS = 4000;
56: 
57: /** Maximum chunk size — hard cap to stay under embedding token budget */
58: const MAX_CHUNK_CHARS = 12000;
59: 
60: /** Minimum file size (chars) to trigger chunking */
61: export const CHUNKING_THRESHOLD = 50000;
62: 
63: /** Max characters for parent summary (BM25 fallback) */
64: const PARENT_SUMMARY_LENGTH = 500;
65: 
66: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67:    3. ANCHOR EXTRACTION
68: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
69: 
70: const ANCHOR_OPEN_RE = /<!--\s*(?:ANCHOR|anchor):\s*([^>\s]+)\s*-->/g;
71: 
72: /**
73:  * Extract anchor sections from content.
74:  * Returns sections between <!-- ANCHOR:id --> and <!-- /ANCHOR:id --> pairs,
75:  * while preserving uncovered text between anchors as unanchored sections.
76:  */
77: function extractAnchorSections(content: string): ExtractedAnchorSections {
78:   const sections: AnchorSection[] = [];
79:   const openPattern = new RegExp(ANCHOR_OPEN_RE.source, 'gi');
80:   let match: RegExpExecArray | null;
81:   let cursor = 0;
82:   let anchorCount = 0;
83: 
84:   while ((match = openPattern.exec(content)) !== null) {
85:     const anchorId = match[1].trim();
86:     const startPos = match.index;
87: 
88:     // Find closing tag
89:     const escapeRegex = (str: string): string => str.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
90:     const closingPattern = new RegExp(
91:       `<!--\\s*/(?:ANCHOR|anchor):\\s*${escapeRegex(anchorId)}\\s*-->`,
92:       'i'
93:     );
94:     const afterOpen = content.slice(startPos + match[0].length);
95:     const closeMatch = closingPattern.exec(afterOpen);
96: 
97:     if (closeMatch) {
98:       if (startPos > cursor) {
99:         const uncovered = content.slice(cursor, startPos);
100:         if (uncovered.trim()) {
101:           sections.push({
102:             id: null,
103:             content: uncovered,
104:             charCount: uncovered.length,
105:           });
106:         }
107:       }
108: 
109:       const endPos = startPos + match[0].length + closeMatch.index + closeMatch[0].length;
110:       const sectionContent = content.slice(startPos, endPos);
111: 
112:       sections.push({
113:         id: anchorId,
114:         content: sectionContent,
115:         charCount: sectionContent.length,
116:       });
117:       anchorCount++;
118:       cursor = endPos;
119:       openPattern.lastIndex = endPos;
120:     }
121:   }
122: 
123:   if (cursor < content.length) {
124:     const trailing = content.slice(cursor);
125:     if (trailing.trim()) {
126:       sections.push({
127:         id: null,
128:         content: trailing,
129:         charCount: trailing.length,
130:       });
131:     }
132:   }
133: 
134:   return { sections, anchorCount };
135: }
136: 
137: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
138:    4. ANCHOR-BASED CHUNKING
139: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
140: 
141: /**
142:  * Group anchor sections into chunks that stay under the target size.
143:  * Small adjacent anchors are merged; large anchors become their own chunk.
144:  */
145: function chunkByAnchors(sections: AnchorSection[]): AnchorChunk[] {
146:   const chunks: AnchorChunk[] = [];
147:   let currentContent = '';
148:   let currentAnchors: string[] = [];
149:   let currentLabels: string[] = [];
150:   let currentSize = 0;
151:   let unanchoredIndex = 0;
152: 
153:   function getSectionLabel(section: AnchorSection): string {
154:     if (section.id) {
155:       return section.id;
156:     }
157:     unanchoredIndex++;
158:     return `unanchored-${unanchoredIndex}`;
159:   }
160: 
161:   function flush(): void {
162:     if (currentContent.trim()) {
163:       const label = currentAnchors.length === 1
164:         ? currentAnchors[0]
165:         : currentAnchors.length > 1
166:           ? `${currentAnchors[0]}..${currentAnchors[currentAnchors.length - 1]}`
167:           : currentLabels.length === 1
168:             ? currentLabels[0]
169:             : `${currentLabels[0]}..${currentLabels[currentLabels.length - 1]}`;
170: 
171:       chunks.push({
172:         content: currentContent.trim(),
173:         anchorIds: [...currentAnchors],
174:         label,
175:         charCount: currentContent.length,
176:       });
177:     }
178:     currentContent = '';
179:     currentAnchors = [];
180:     currentLabels = [];
181:     currentSize = 0;
182:   }
183: 
184:   for (const section of sections) {
185:     const sectionLabel = getSectionLabel(section);
186: 
187:     // Oversized anchor sections must be further split to respect
188:     // MAX_CHUNK_CHARS. Previously they were emitted as single oversized chunks.
189:     if (section.charCount > MAX_CHUNK_CHARS) {
190:       flush();
191:       // Split oversized content into MAX_CHUNK_CHARS-sized sub-chunks
192:       const lines = section.content.split('\n');
193:       let subContent = '';
194:       let subIdx = 0;
195:       for (const line of lines) {
196:         if (subContent.length + line.length + 1 > MAX_CHUNK_CHARS && subContent.trim()) {
197:           chunks.push({
198:             content: subContent.trim(),
199:             anchorIds: section.id ? [section.id] : [],
200:             label: section.id ? `${section.id}:${subIdx}` : `${sectionLabel}:${subIdx}`,
201:             charCount: subContent.length,
202:           });
203:           subContent = '';
204:           subIdx++;
205:         }
206:         // Handle single lines exceeding MAX_CHUNK_CHARS: split at char boundary
207:         if (line.length > MAX_CHUNK_CHARS && !subContent) {
208:           for (let c = 0; c < line.length; c += MAX_CHUNK_CHARS) {
209:             const slice = line.slice(c, c + MAX_CHUNK_CHARS);
210:             chunks.push({
211:               content: slice,
212:               anchorIds: section.id ? [section.id] : [],
213:               label: section.id ? `${section.id}:${subIdx}` : `${sectionLabel}:${subIdx}`,
214:               charCount: slice.length,
215:             });
216:             subIdx++;
217:           }
218:           continue;
219:         }
220:         subContent += (subContent ? '\n' : '') + line;
221:       }
222:       if (subContent.trim()) {
223:         chunks.push({
224:           content: subContent.trim(),
225:           anchorIds: section.id ? [section.id] : [],
226:           label: subIdx > 0
227:             ? (section.id ? `${section.id}:${subIdx}` : `${sectionLabel}:${subIdx}`)
228:             : sectionLabel,
229:           charCount: subContent.length,
230:         });
231:       }
232:       continue;
233:     }
234: 
235:     // Would adding this section exceed the target?
236:     if (currentSize + section.charCount > TARGET_CHUNK_CHARS && currentContent.trim()) {
237:       flush();
238:     }
239: 
240:     currentContent += (currentContent ? '\n\n' : '') + section.content;
241:     currentLabels.push(sectionLabel);
242:     if (section.id) {
243:       currentAnchors.push(section.id);
244:     }
245:     currentSize += section.charCount;
246:   }
247: 
248:   flush();
249:   return chunks;
250: }
251: 
252: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
253:    5. STRUCTURE-BASED FALLBACK CHUNKING
254: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
255: 
256: /**
257:  * Fall back to splitting by markdown headings and paragraphs.
258:  * Uses the shared AST-aware markdown chunker so fenced code and tables remain atomic.
259:  */
260: function chunkByStructure(content: string): AnchorChunk[] {
261:   const maxTokens = Math.floor(TARGET_CHUNK_CHARS / 4);
262: 
263:   return chunkMarkdown(content, maxTokens).map((chunk, index) => ({
264:     content: chunk.content,
265:     anchorIds: [],
266:     label: `chunk-${index + 1}`,
267:     charCount: chunk.content.length,
268:   }));
269: }
270: 
271: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
272:    6. MAIN ENTRY POINT
273: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
274: 
275: /**
276:  * Chunk a large memory file into smaller pieces for indexing.
277:  *
278:  * Strategy:
279:  * 1. Try anchor-based splitting first (files with ANCHOR tags)
280:  * 2. Fall back to structure-aware markdown splitting
281:  *
282:  * @param content - The full file content
283:  * @returns ChunkingResult with strategy info and chunk array
284:  */
285: export function chunkLargeFile(content: string): ChunkingResult {
286:   const parentSummary = content.slice(0, PARENT_SUMMARY_LENGTH).trim();
287: 
288:   // Try anchor-based chunking first
289:   const { sections: anchorSections, anchorCount } = extractAnchorSections(content);
290: 
291:   if (anchorCount >= 2) {
292:     const chunks = chunkByAnchors(anchorSections);
293:     if (chunks.length >= 2) {
294:       return { strategy: 'anchor', chunks, parentSummary };
295:     }
296:   }
297: 
298:   // Fall back to structure-based chunking
299:   const chunks = chunkByStructure(content);
300:   return { strategy: 'structure', chunks, parentSummary };
301: }
302: 
303: /**
304:  * Check if a file's content exceeds the chunking threshold.
305:  */
306: export function needsChunking(content: string): boolean {
307:   return content.length > CHUNKING_THRESHOLD;
308: }

76: function sortRows<TPayload extends JsonValue>(rows: readonly TargetStateRow<TPayload>[]): TargetStateRow<TPayload>[] {

79:     return targetCompare !== 0 ? targetCompare : left.key.localeCompare(right.key);
80:   });

83: function stableJson(value: JsonValue): string {
84:   if (value === null || typeof value !== 'object') {
85:     return JSON.stringify(value);
86:   }
87: 
88:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
89:     return `[${value.map((item) => stableJson(item)).join(',')}]`;
90:   }
91: 
92:   const entries = Object.entries(value).sort(([left], [right]) => left.localeCompare(right));
93:   return `{${entries.map(([key, item]) => `${JSON.stringify(key)}:${stableJson(item)}`).join(',')}}`;

97:   if (!row) {

110: function createAction<TPayload extends JsonValue>(

113:   key: string,
114:   prior: TargetStateRow<TPayload> | null,
115:   desired: TargetStateRow<TPayload> | null,

132: export function planStatediff<TPayload extends JsonValue>(

134:   priorRows: readonly TargetStateRow<TPayload>[],
135:   options: PlanStatediffOptions,
136: ): StatediffAction<TPayload>[] {

138:   const priorById = new Map(sortRows(priorRows).map((row) => [rowId(row), row]));
139:   const ids = [...new Set([...desiredById.keys(), ...priorById.keys()])].sort();
140:   const actions: StatediffAction<TPayload>[] = [];
141: 
142:   for (const id of ids) {
143:     const desired = desiredById.get(id) ?? null;
144:     const prior = priorById.get(id) ?? null;
145:     const reference = desired ?? prior;
146:     if (!reference) {
147:       continue;
148:     }
149: 
150:     if (desired && !prior) {
151:       actions.push(createAction(options.incompletePrior ? 'upsert' : 'insert', reference.target, reference.key, null, desired, options));
152:       continue;
153:     }
154: 
155:     if (!desired && prior) {
156:       actions.push(createAction('delete', reference.target, reference.key, prior, null, options));
157:       continue;
158:     }
159: 
160:     if (desired && prior && stateHash(desired) !== stateHash(prior)) {
161:       actions.push(createAction(options.incompletePrior ? 'upsert' : 'replace', reference.target, reference.key, prior, desired, options));
162:     }
163:   }

165:   return actions;

170:   composite: CompositeTarget<TPayload>,

176: export function createStatediffAction<TPayload extends JsonValue>(
177:   action: DiffActionKind,
178:   params: {
179:     readonly target: StatediffTargetType;
180:     readonly key: string;
181:     readonly sourceOperation: string;
182:     readonly prior?: TargetStateRow<TPayload> | null;
183:     readonly desired?: TargetStateRow<TPayload> | null;

196:     newStateHash: params.newStateHash ?? stateHash(desired),

210:   const ordered = [...actions].sort((left, right) => {
211:     const targetCompare = String(left.target).localeCompare(String(right.target));
212:     return targetCompare !== 0 ? targetCompare : left.key.localeCompare(right.key);
213:   });
214: 
215:   for (const action of ordered) {
216:     const sink = sinkByTarget.get(action.target);
217:     if (!sink) {
218:       throw new Error(`No statediff sink registered for target "${action.target}"`);
219:     }
220:     await sink.apply(action);
221:   }
222: 
223:   return {
224:     sourceOperation: ordered[0]?.sourceOperation ?? 'unknown',
225:     actions: ordered,
226:   };
227: }
228: 
229: /** Runs action subscribers after durable sinks have applied. */
230: export async function notifyStatediffSubscribers<TPayload extends JsonValue>(
231:   actions: readonly StatediffAction<TPayload>[],
232:   subscribers: readonly StatediffSubscriber<TPayload>[],
233: ): Promise<StatediffSubscriberResult[]> {

242:     } catch (error: unknown) {

244:       reports.push({ name: subscriber.name, actionCount: actions.length, ok: false, error: message });

4: import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';

20:   receiptKey: string;
21:   contentHash: string | null;
22:   requestFingerprintHash: string;
23:   payloadHash: string;
24: }

26: export type IdempotencyLookupResult =
27:   | { status: 'miss'; key: IdempotencyReceiptKey }
28:   | { status: 'replay'; key: IdempotencyReceiptKey; response: MCPResponse }
29:   | { status: 'conflict'; key: IdempotencyReceiptKey; storedPayloadHash: string };
30: 
31: interface ReceiptRow {
32:   payload_hash: string;
33:   response_payload: string;
34: }

36: const CLIENT_TOKEN_KEYS = new Set([
37:   'idempotencykey',
38:   'idempotency_key',
39:   'idempotencytoken',
40:   'idempotency_token',
41:   'clientidempotencykey',
42:   'client_idempotency_key',
43:   'clientidempotencytoken',
44:   'client_idempotency_token',
45: ]);

47: export function isMemoryIdempotencyEnabled(): boolean {
48:   const raw = process.env.SPECKIT_MEMORY_IDEMPOTENCY?.trim().toLowerCase();
49:   return raw === '1' || raw === 'true' || raw === 'yes' || raw === 'on';
50: }
51: 
52: function normalizeForHash(value: unknown): unknown {
53:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
54:     return value.map(normalizeForHash);
55:   }
56:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object') {
57:     return value;
58:   }
59: 
60:   const record = value as Record<string, unknown>;
61:   const normalized: Record<string, unknown> = {};
62:   for (const key of Object.keys(record).sort()) {
63:     if (CLIENT_TOKEN_KEYS.has(key.toLowerCase())) {
64:       continue;
65:     }
66:     const normalizedValue = normalizeForHash(record[key]);
67:     if (normalizedValue !== undefined) {
68:       normalized[key] = normalizedValue;
69:     }
70:   }
71:   return normalized;
72: }
73: 
74: function hashJson(value: unknown): string {
75:   return createHash('sha256')
76:     .update(JSON.stringify(normalizeForHash(value)))
77:     .digest('hex');
78: }

80: export function deriveIdempotencyReceiptKey(input: IdempotencyReceiptInput): IdempotencyReceiptKey {
81:   const requestFingerprintHash = hashJson(input.requestFingerprint);
82:   const payloadHash = hashJson(input.payload);

85:     contentHash: input.contentHash ?? null,

88:   return {
89:     operation: input.operation,
90:     receiptKey: hashJson(material),
91:     contentHash: input.contentHash ?? null,
92:     requestFingerprintHash,
93:     payloadHash,

95: }

97: function addReplayMarker(response: MCPResponse): MCPResponse {
98:   const cloned = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(response)) as MCPResponse;
99:   const first = cloned.content?.[0];
100:   if (!first || first.type !== 'text' || typeof first.text !== 'string') {
101:     return cloned;
102:   }
103: 
104:   try {
105:     const envelope = JSON.parse(first.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
106:     const data = envelope.data && typeof envelope.data === 'object' && !Array.isArray(envelope.data)
107:       ? envelope.data as Record<string, unknown>
108:       : {};
109:     data.replayed = true;
110:     envelope.data = data;
111:     const hints = Array.isArray(envelope.hints) ? envelope.hints : [];
112:     envelope.hints = ['Replayed prior idempotent write result', ...hints];
113:     const meta = envelope.meta && typeof envelope.meta === 'object' && !Array.isArray(envelope.meta)
114:       ? envelope.meta as Record<string, unknown>
115:       : {};
116:     meta.replayed = true;
117:     envelope.meta = meta;
118:     first.text = JSON.stringify(envelope, null, 2);
119:   } catch {
120:     return cloned;
121:   }
122: 
123:   return cloned;

126: export function lookupIdempotencyReceipt(
127:   database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
128:   input: IdempotencyReceiptInput,

130:   const key = deriveIdempotencyReceiptKey(input);
131:   const row = database.prepare(`
132:     SELECT payload_hash, response_payload
133:     FROM memory_idempotency_receipts
134:     WHERE receipt_key = ?
135:   `).get(key.receiptKey) as ReceiptRow | undefined;
136: 
137:   if (!row) {
138:     return { status: 'miss', key };
139:   }
140:   if (row.payload_hash !== key.payloadHash) {
141:     return { status: 'conflict', key, storedPayloadHash: row.payload_hash };
142:   }
143:   return {
144:     status: 'replay',
145:     key,
146:     response: addReplayMarker(JSON.parse(row.response_payload) as MCPResponse),

151:   database: BetterSqlite3.Database,

161:       request_fingerprint,
162:       payload_hash,
163:       response_payload,
164:       memory_id,
165:       updated_at
166:     ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
167:     ON CONFLICT(receipt_key) DO UPDATE SET
168:       response_payload = excluded.response_payload,
169:       memory_id = excluded.memory_id,
170:       updated_at = datetime('now')
171:   `).run(
172:     key.receiptKey,
173:     key.operation,
174:     key.contentHash,
175:     key.requestFingerprintHash,
176:     key.payloadHash,
177:     JSON.stringify(response),
178:     memoryId ?? null,
179:   );

182: export function extractMemoryIdFromResponse(response: MCPResponse): number | null {

190:     return typeof id === 'number' && Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
191:   } catch {
192:     return null;
193:   }
194: }

29: function normalizeScopeValue(value: unknown): string | null {
30:   return typeof value === 'string' && value.length > 0 ? value : null;
31: }
32: 
33: function scopeMatches(row: CandidateRow, scope: MemoryScopeMatch): boolean {
34:   return normalizeScopeValue(row.tenant_id) === normalizeScopeValue(scope.tenantId)
35:     && normalizeScopeValue(row.user_id) === normalizeScopeValue(scope.userId)
36:     && normalizeScopeValue(row.agent_id) === normalizeScopeValue(scope.agentId)
37:     && normalizeScopeValue(row.session_id) === normalizeScopeValue(scope.sessionId);
38: }

40: export function parseNearDuplicateHint(raw: unknown): NearDuplicateHint | null {
41:   if (typeof raw !== 'string' || raw.length === 0) {
42:     return null;
43:   }
44:   try {
45:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial<NearDuplicateHint>;
46:     if (
47:       typeof parsed.id === 'number'
48:       && Number.isInteger(parsed.id)
49:       && parsed.id > 0
50:       && typeof parsed.similarity === 'number'
51:       && Number.isFinite(parsed.similarity)
52:       && typeof parsed.threshold === 'number'
53:       && Number.isFinite(parsed.threshold)
54:     ) {
55:       return { id: parsed.id, similarity: parsed.similarity, threshold: parsed.threshold };
56:     }
57:   } catch {
58:     return null;
59:   }
60:   return null;
61: }

63: export function shouldSkipNearDuplicateCheck(database: BetterSqlite3.Database, memoryId: number): boolean {
64:   const row = database.prepare(`
65:     SELECT updated_at, last_dedup_checked_at
66:     FROM memory_index
67:     WHERE id = ?
68:   `).get(memoryId) as Pick<CandidateRow, 'updated_at' | 'last_dedup_checked_at'> | undefined;
69:   if (!row?.last_dedup_checked_at) {
70:     return false;
71:   }
72:   if (!row.updated_at) {
73:     return true;
74:   }
75:   return new Date(row.updated_at).getTime() <= new Date(row.last_dedup_checked_at).getTime();

77: 
78: export function clearNearDuplicateCheck(database: BetterSqlite3.Database, memoryId: number): void {
79:   database.prepare(`

81:     SET near_duplicate_of = NULL,
82:         last_dedup_checked_at = NULL
83:     WHERE id = ?

87: export function recordNearDuplicateCheck(args: {
88:   database: BetterSqlite3.Database;
89:   memoryId: number;
90:   specFolder: string;
91:   contentHash: string | null;
92:   embedding: Float32Array | null;
93:   scope?: MemoryScopeMatch;
94: }): NearDuplicateHint | null {
95:   if (!isMemoryIdempotencyEnabled() || !args.embedding) {
96:     return null;
97:   }
98:   if (shouldSkipNearDuplicateCheck(args.database, args.memoryId)) {
99:     const row = args.database.prepare('SELECT near_duplicate_of FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?')
100:       .get(args.memoryId) as { near_duplicate_of: string | null } | undefined;
101:     return parseNearDuplicateHint(row?.near_duplicate_of);
102:   }
103: 
104:   const candidates = vectorIndex.vectorSearch(args.embedding, {
105:     limit: 8,
106:     specFolder: args.specFolder,
107:     minSimilarity: Math.round(NEAR_DUPLICATE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD * 100),
108:     includeConstitutional: false,
109:   }) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
110:   const ids = candidates
111:     .map((candidate) => candidate.id)
112:     .filter((id): id is number => typeof id === 'number' && Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 && id !== args.memoryId);
113:   const rows = ids.length > 0
114:     ? args.database.prepare(`
115:         SELECT id, content_hash, tenant_id, user_id, agent_id, session_id, updated_at,
116:                last_dedup_checked_at, near_duplicate_of
117:         FROM memory_index
118:         WHERE id IN (${ids.map(() => '?').join(',')})
119:       `).all(...ids) as CandidateRow[]
120:     : [];
121:   const rowsById = new Map(rows.map((row) => [row.id, row]));
122: 
123:   let hint: NearDuplicateHint | null = null;
124:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
125:     const id = typeof candidate.id === 'number' ? candidate.id : null;
126:     if (id == null || id === args.memoryId) {
127:       continue;
128:     }
129:     const row = rowsById.get(id);
130:     if (!row || !scopeMatches(row, args.scope ?? {})) {
131:       continue;
132:     }
133:     if (args.contentHash && row.content_hash === args.contentHash) {
134:       continue;
135:     }
136:     const rawSimilarity = typeof candidate.similarity === 'number' ? candidate.similarity : 0;
137:     const similarity = rawSimilarity > 1 ? rawSimilarity / 100 : rawSimilarity;
138:     if (similarity >= NEAR_DUPLICATE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD) {
139:       hint = { id, similarity, threshold: NEAR_DUPLICATE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD };
140:       break;
141:     }
142:   }
143: 
144:   args.database.prepare(`
145:     UPDATE memory_index
146:     SET near_duplicate_of = ?,
147:         last_dedup_checked_at = datetime('now')
148:     WHERE id = ?
149:   `).run(hint ? JSON.stringify(hint) : null, args.memoryId);

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Canonical Code Graph DB Directory
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: import { existsSync, mkdirSync, realpathSync } from 'node:fs';
6: import { dirname, resolve } from 'node:path';
7: import { isWithinWorkspace } from './utils/workspace-path.js';
8: 
9: export class CanonicalDbDirError extends Error {
10:   readonly code: string | undefined;
11: 
12:   constructor(message: string, code?: string) {
13:     super(message);
14:     this.name = 'CanonicalDbDirError';
15:     this.code = code;
16:   }
17: }
18: 
19: export function resolveCanonicalDbDir(dir: string, workspaceRoot?: string): string {
20:   const resolvedDir = resolve(dir);
21: 
22:   try {
23:     let canonicalWorkspace: string | null = null;
24:     if (workspaceRoot) {
25:       const resolvedWorkspace = resolve(workspaceRoot);
26:       if (!isWithinWorkspace(resolvedWorkspace, resolvedDir)) {
27:         throw new CanonicalDbDirError(
28:           `Code graph DB directory must stay within the workspace root: ${resolvedWorkspace}`,
29:           'OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE',
30:         );
31:       }
32:       canonicalWorkspace = realpathSync.native(resolvedWorkspace);
33:     }
34:     // Resolve symlinks of the deepest EXISTING ancestor and reject an out-of-workspace
35:     // escape BEFORE mkdir, so a symlink-escaping path never creates directories outside the
36:     // workspace. (The lexical pre-check above does not resolve symlinks; the post-mkdir check
37:     // below stays as defense-in-depth against a TOCTOU symlink swap.)
38:     if (canonicalWorkspace) {
39:       let ancestor = resolvedDir;
40:       while (!existsSync(ancestor)) {
41:         const parent = dirname(ancestor);
42:         if (parent === ancestor) break;
43:         ancestor = parent;
44:       }
45:       const canonicalAncestor = realpathSync.native(ancestor);
46:       if (!isWithinWorkspace(canonicalWorkspace, canonicalAncestor)) {
47:         throw new CanonicalDbDirError(
48:           `Code graph DB directory must stay within the workspace root: ${canonicalWorkspace}`,
49:           'OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE',
50:         );
51:       }
52:     }
53:     mkdirSync(resolvedDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
54:     const canonicalDir = realpathSync.native(resolvedDir);
55:     if (canonicalWorkspace && !isWithinWorkspace(canonicalWorkspace, canonicalDir)) {
56:       throw new CanonicalDbDirError(
57:         `Code graph DB directory must stay within the workspace root: ${canonicalWorkspace}`,
58:         'OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE',
59:       );
60:     }
61:     return canonicalDir;
62:   } catch (error: unknown) {
63:     if (error instanceof CanonicalDbDirError) {
64:       throw error;
65:     }
66:     const code = error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error
67:       ? String(error.code)
68:       : undefined;
69:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
70:     throw new CanonicalDbDirError(`Unable to resolve canonical code graph DB directory ${resolvedDir}: ${message}`, code);
71:   }
72: }
